canonical_definition: The ability of a model to learn to solve difficult problems by training on solutions to simpler problems, without requiring direct supervision on the harder instances.
papers: Generalizing from SIMPLE to HARD Visual Reasoning: Can We Mitigate Modality Imbalance in VLMs?
Self-Improving Transformers Overcome Easy-to-Hard and Length Generalization Challenges

definitions.text: The ability of a model to learn to solve difficult problems by training on solutions to simpler problems, without requiring direct supervision on the harder instances.
definitions.paper: Self-Improving Transformers Overcome Easy-to-Hard and Length Generalization Challenges

definitions.text: The latent ability of a model to generalize from training on simpler instances of a task to performing well on more complex, unseen instances, particularly involving longer or more intricate reasoning chains.
definitions.paper: Generalizing from SIMPLE to HARD Visual Reasoning: Can We Mitigate Modality Imbalance in VLMs?

relationships.paper: Easy-to-Hard Generalization: Scalable Alignment Beyond Human Supervision

neurips24: Easy-to-Hard Generalization: Scalable Alignment Beyond Human Supervision

relationships.paper: Generalizing from SIMPLE to HARD Visual Reasoning: Can We Mitigate Modality Imbalance in VLMs?
relationships.evidence: Our findings suggest that self-improvement is not limited to length generalization but also enables easy-to-hard generalization, where a model trained on simpler tasks successfully learns harder tasks without additional supervision. (Section 1)

icml25: Generalizing from SIMPLE to HARD Visual Reasoning: Can We Mitigate Modality Imbalance in VLMs?

relationships.paper: Generalizing from SIMPLE to HARD Visual Reasoning: Can We Mitigate Modality Imbalance in VLMs?
relationships.evidence: We observe that current models can S2H generalize on text – when trained to read short sequences from small LaTeX-formatted tables, the models can read longer paths from larger tables, also provided in LaTeX code. However, they fail to length-generalize on images. To address the generalization gap and imbalanced learning of different modalities, our goal is to transfer the generalization behavior from text to image modality. (Figure 1)
